articles.headline: End Of An Era: Why The FAA Just Banned SFO’s Iconic Parallel Landings
articles.blurb: No more wingtip-to-wingtip flying over the bay.
articles.takeaway: San Francisco's legendary parallel landings, a procedural marvel that maximized runway capacity during peak operations, are being eliminated due to evolving safety standards and modern aircraft performance capabilities.

articles.headline: Why The Boeing 737 MAX Remains So Popular
articles.blurb: Boeing's latest narrowbody aircraft continues to be a commercial success.
articles.takeaway: Despite past safety crises and lengthy grounding, the 737 MAX's enduring demand reflects airlines' confidence in Boeing's remediation efforts and their need for fuel-efficient capacity.

articles.headline: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
articles.blurb: The February 2026 eruption at Piton de la Fournaise has lasted longer and produced a larger volume of lava than recent eruptions from this frequently active volcano.
articles.takeaway: Volcanic eruptions creating new coastlines can alter airfield drainage systems and ash dispersal patterns, requiring operational adjustments for nearby aviation facilities.

articles.headline: Artemis II launches successfully on first crewed US Moon mission since Apollo

articles.takeaway: Artemis II's successful launch validates NASA's deep-space architecture for sustained lunar operations, establishing the technical foundation for future commercial lunar logistics partnerships.

articles.headline: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
articles.blurb: Spurred by American ingenuity, astronauts on NASA’s Artemis II mission are in flight, preparing for the first crewed lunar flyby in more than 50 years. NASA’s SLS (Space Launch System) rocket lifted off from Launch Pad 39B at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida at 6:35 p.m. EDT Wednesday,...
articles.takeaway: Artemis II's successful SLS launch restarts crewed lunar exploration architecture, validating heavy-lift capabilities essential for sustained deep-space missions and lunar base development.

articles.headline: How to Assess Safety Risk
articles.blurb: A practical walkthrough of safety risk assessment under 14 CFR Part 5.
articles.takeaway: Part 5's safety risk assessment framework provides operators a systematic methodology to identify and quantify hazards before incidents occur, shifting from reactive to proactive safety management.

articles.headline: Backtrack? Why American Airlines Is Worried Over Delta & United’s Passenger Experience Investments
articles.blurb: The carrier is concerned by its key competitors in big ways.
articles.takeaway: American Airlines faces competitive pressure as Delta and United's elevated service investments risk eroding market share among premium leisure and business travelers.

articles.headline: Blue Airbus A330: SAS Unveils Special Livery To Commemorate 80 Years Of Flying

articles.headline: Artemis 2 launches on first human mission to the moon in more than 50 years
articles.blurb: The first human mission beyond Earth orbit in more than 50 years is underway with an April 1 launch of four astronauts on a flight around the moon.
articles.takeaway: Artemis 2's crewed lunar flyby represents NASA's operational validation of deep-space human spaceflight systems before landing missions, establishing critical infrastructure for sustained lunar operations.

articles.headline: This Is How Much A Boeing 747 Freighter Costs In 2026
articles.blurb: How has the aircraft's list price changed over the years?
articles.takeaway: Boeing 747F list prices have remained relatively stable despite inflation, reflecting limited competition in the large widebody freighter segment and sustained cargo demand.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk
articles.blurb: Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).
articles.takeaway: Organizations must document how they selected specific risk controls and justified acceptance of remaining residual risk to demonstrate compliance with regulatory requirements.

articles.headline: American Eagle Bombardier CRJ700 Diverts To Philadelphia Due To Nose Gear Steering Issues
articles.blurb: The incident is certainly cause for concern.

articles.takeaway: RAF Wittering's new commander oversees 1,400 personnel at a critical flight-training hub, signaling institutional focus on leadership culture alongside operational excellence.

articles.headline: Here’s When Delta Air Lines Plans To Debut The Largest Boeing 787 Dreamliner
articles.blurb: Delta's Dreamliners will serve long-haul routes across the globe.
articles.takeaway: Delta's 787-10 deployment targets premium long-haul capacity expansion, signaling major carriers' shift toward larger wide-body aircraft to improve unit economics on international routes.

articles.headline: Airbus Tests Armed ‘Bird of Prey’ Interceptor Drone

articles.headline: From the Flight Deck – Eastern Iowa Airport, IA (CID)

articles.takeaway: Regional airports like CID drive pilot training pipelines by hosting flight schools alongside commercial operations, creating a talent development ecosystem for the broader aviation industry.

articles.headline: USAF E-3 AWACS Loss in Saudi Arabia Puts Spotlight on E-7 Wedgetail Procurement

The loss of a U.S. Air Force E-3G Sentry AWACS (Airborne Warning and Control System) following the drone and missile attack at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi...
articles.takeaway: The E-3 loss accelerates the Air Force's transition to the E-7 Wedgetail, a more survivable platform with advanced threat detection capabilities against modern drone and missile threats.

articles.headline: SpaceX files for IPO, opening Musk’s Moon and Mars push to investors

articles.headline: SpaceX quietly files for big bang IPO

articles.headline: F-35 Crash in Nevada Leaves Pilot With Minor Injuries
articles.blurb: Air Force says the aircraft went down on restricted federal property north of Las Vegas.
articles.takeaway: The incident's occurrence on restricted federal property suggests containment protocols worked as designed, limiting potential damage to populated areas and simplifying recovery operations.

articles.headline: Just 64 Miles: Delta Air Lines' 10 New Ultra-Short Flights Revealed
articles.blurb: How many of them can you guess? And to think: Delta had even shorter flights in the past.
articles.takeaway: Delta's ultra-short regional routes suggest airlines are monetizing thin-margin point-to-point markets previously considered unviable, signaling a shift in network strategy.

articles.headline: At Top Speed, This Is How Fighter Jets Like The F-35 Perform
articles.blurb: Fighter jets perform poorly at their theoretical max speeds which is why they almost never fly at those speeds.
articles.takeaway: Fighter jets operate well below maximum speed ratings due to fuel consumption, structural stress, and thermal limits, making supersonic dash capability more theoretical than operational.

articles.headline: Mesa Gateway Adds Light Aircraft Landing Fee

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offers greater near-term environmental gains than waiting for new aircraft technologies alone.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Why Bigger Aircraft Are Sometimes Less Efficient
articles.blurb: Do you know why this can be the case?
articles.takeaway: Larger aircraft's efficiency gains from economies of scale are often offset by increased structural weight, aerodynamic drag, and airport infrastructure constraints that reduce their per-seat fuel efficiency compared to optimally-sized regional jets.

articles.headline: Cirrus Launches New Simulator Training Program For SR Series Owners
articles.blurb: Mission Ready Simulator Sessions is a free, scenario-based training program for SR20 and SR22 owners.
articles.takeaway: Free simulator training reduces owner liability and insurance costs while standardizing emergency procedures across Cirrus's installed fleet.

articles.headline: NASA’s X-59 returns to flight after false cockpit warning

articles.headline: Artemis 2’s (nearly) 10-day flight around the moon
articles.blurb: The Artemis 2 mission will send humans to the vicinity of the moon for the first time in more than 50 years on a mission lasting almost 10 days.
articles.takeaway: Artemis 2's extended lunar trajectory demands advanced life support and radiation shielding systems that will establish critical operational baselines for deep-space human missions beyond Earth orbit.

articles.headline: Saltzman: Space ‘baked into’ modern combat operations

articles.headline: FCC eyes sweeping reforms to boost US drone power

articles.headline: FAA Restricts SFO Landings
